title: Last week marked the end of DSA4262 at the National University of Singapore: a module focused on data-driven digital mental health intervention design.
post:
Last week marked the end of DSA4262 at the National University of Singapore: a module focused on data-driven digital mental health intervention design.

Working in mental health can be challenging and humbling for data scientists. We often don't have all the data we would like to have, and things are rarely clear cut. For example, how would you operationalize and measure loneliness?

Over the semester, the 60 statistics and data science student teams worked through a full data-driven product cycle: identifying a meaningful mental health problem, framing it with a Jobs To Be Done lens, designing an intervention, building a prototype, choosing success metrics, stress-testing their assumptions, and reflecting on clinical and ethical implications. The final pitch night brought it all together in front of an external panel.

I'm super proud of all students for pushing through the ambiguity and showing enthusiasm and resilience.
